title: Ellie Ross Mrs Gary Breath
description: Annie Hughes is happily single, gainfully employed and financially... fine. But all that changes on the night of her 34th birthday when her parents make her an offer she can't refuse. Even if she really, definitely should. Part romcom, part social autopsy, this brand new solo play from Ellie Ross (BBC Radio 5 Live, i Paper's 50 Best Jokes of the Fringe, Leicester Square Theatre's SketchOff!) satirises our obsession with marriage and ticking life's boxes. Big-hearted, big-character storytelling about the cost of fitting in - and the freedom of opting out.

venue.name: Junk Poets at Caravanserai
venue.address: North of St Peters Church, York Place, East Sussex

reviews.reviewer: By The Reviews Hub - Brighton May 8, 2026 Updated: May 8, 2026 No Comments 3 Min
reviews.rating_raw: The Reviews Hub Star Rating 80 % 80% A deft comic monologue
reviews.excerpt: Annie Hughes, as played by writer/performer Ellie Ross, is cheerfuly meandering through life when her posh parents make her an offer she should probably refuse. If she gets married by the age of 35, they’ll give her £150,000. This is an hour of tightly written, well-paced comic monologue.
